tealcode.heroSummary: Open-source desktop IDE with integrated AI: professional Cursor/VS Code-like interface, portfolio aesthetic and an agent with Ask, Agent and Plan modes.
tealcode.context: I wanted a local, free coding assistant without expensive subscriptions. PauStudio combines Tauri (native Rust, ~30 MB), Monaco Editor and a ReAct agent with file read/write tools. Supports Ollama (Qwen3-Coder, DeepSeek-R1) and free-tier APIs (DeepSeek, Qwen DashScope, GLM-4.7-Flash).
tealcode.overview: Open any project folder, edit with syntax highlighting, chat with the agent and review diffs before applying changes. Integrated terminal, Ctrl+S/P/` shortcuts, direct download from the portfolio.

highlights.title: VS Code-like workbench
highlights.text: File explorer, multi-tab editor, custom teal Monaco theme and native PTY terminal.

highlights.title: Tool-using agent
highlights.text: ReAct loop with list_files, read_file, search_files and write_file. Mandatory diff preview before saving.

highlights.title: Free Chinese AI
highlights.text: Local Ollama (qwen3-coder-next) or DeepSeek, Qwen and GLM APIs with free tiers. User provides API key.

highlights.title: Lightweight desktop app
highlights.text: Tauri 2 instead of Electron: small installer, real filesystem access, Windows/macOS/Linux builds via GitHub Actions.

tealcode.role: Design, full-stack development and IDE distribution as a downloadable portfolio project.
tealcode.caseStudyTitle: Case study: PauStudio — Local AI IDE
tealcode.caseStudyIntro: PauStudio (TealCode) is an open-source desktop IDE combining my portfolio aesthetics with AI agent capabilities comparable to Cursor, but running locally with free models.
tealcode.caseStudyProblem: Cloud code assistants require expensive subscriptions and send your code to external servers. As a student and developer, I needed a local, lightweight, free alternative that could read and modify real project files without depending on a SaaS.
tealcode.caseStudySolution: Built PauStudio with Tauri 2 (Rust) for a ~30 MB native binary, Monaco Editor for the editing experience, and a ReAct agent with filesystem tools (list, read, search, write). Supports local Ollama (Qwen3-Coder, DeepSeek-R1) and free-tier APIs (DeepSeek, Qwen DashScope, GLM-4.7-Flash). Mandatory diff preview before applying changes ensures user control.
tealcode.caseStudyResults: Functional IDE downloadable from the portfolio with Windows/macOS/Linux installers via GitHub Actions. Integrated PTY terminal, file explorer, multi-file tabs and Ask/Agent/Plan modes. Demonstrable project for recruiters seeking AI + desktop profile.

study-agents.heroSummary: Capstone of the Developer 10X with AI program (Instituto de Inteligencia Artificial): 8 intensive weeks + final project. Multi-agent platform in production inside my portfolio — real study flows, not long chat paragraphs.
study-agents.context: Study Agents is the final project of the Developer 10X with Artificial Intelligence course at the Instituto de Inteligencia Artificial — an 8-week program plus capstone where I consolidated agents, RAG, Python APIs and a Next.js product. I built on what we learned in class (LLM orchestration, pipelines, best practices) and went further on my own: full multi-agent architecture, study-first UX and a real deployment linked from this portfolio. It is my flagship applied-AI piece solving a problem I lived as a student.
study-agents.overview: The goal: turn AI into an interactive study companion. Users upload PDFs, content is indexed with RAG (ChromaDB) and eight specialized agents cover the full loop — explain at the right level (0–10), answer with context, generate tests and exercises, correct with educational feedback and export notes. FastAPI + LangChain on the backend, Next.js on the frontend, Google login and a public demo recruiters can try in one click.

highlights.title: From 10X course to live product
highlights.text: Turned the Instituto de IA capstone into a deployed, maintained app — not a mockup: production code, video demo and a direct link from projects.

highlights.title: 8 orchestrated agents
highlights.text: Content Processor, Explanation, Q&A, Test Generator, Feedback, Exercise Generator/Corrector and Correction Agent, shared memory and smart GPT-3.5 / GPT-4 routing.

highlights.title: RAG on your notes
highlights.text: PDFs → chunking → embeddings → ChromaDB. Answers ground on the user's material, not generic hallucinations.

highlights.title: Complete study flows
highlights.text: Chat, Markdown→PDF notes, adaptive tests, language flashcards, in-browser code runner and progress dashboard.

highlights.title: In production
highlights.text: paupedrejon.com/study-agents · NextAuth (Google) · per-user API keys · scalable FastAPI backend.

highlights.title: Portfolio integration
highlights.text: Same Next.js 15 monorepo, i18n, consistent branding and visible CTAs on home and projects.

study-agents.stackIntro: Clear split: Python orchestrates AI; React/Next.js delivers the experience.
study-agents.role: Sole capstone owner: multi-agent architecture, Python backend, Next.js frontend, RAG, portfolio integration, deployment, documentation (README + demo) and UX iteration based on real student use.
study-agents.caseStudyTitle: Case study: Study Agents — AI learning platform
study-agents.caseStudyIntro: Study Agents transforms PDFs and notes into a personal tutor with eight specialized agents, ChromaDB RAG and complete study flows: chat, tests, exercises and note export.
study-agents.caseStudyProblem: Students use generic AI chats that don't know their material, don't adapt explanations to their level (0-10) and don't offer a structured study cycle with tests, correction and progress tracking. As a UPC student, I experienced this daily.
study-agents.caseStudySolution: Multi-agent architecture with FastAPI + LangChain: Content Processor indexes PDFs in ChromaDB, Explanation Agent adapts to user level, Q&A answers with RAG context, Test/Exercise Generators create personalized assessments, and Feedback/Correction Agents provide educational feedback. Next.js 15 frontend with NextAuth (Google), per-user API keys and public demo integrated in the portfolio.
study-agents.caseStudyResults: Platform in production at paupedrejon.com/study-agents, accessible with one click from the portfolio. Capstone of the 10X AI Developer program at the Artificial Intelligence Institute. Demonstrable for recruiters: not a mockup, maintained code with scalable backend.

turelojya.heroSummary: Production full-stack luxury watch e-commerce: catalog, valuation engine over 100,000+ market records, Redsys checkout, custom backoffice and multilingual technical SEO — built end-to-end at Owius.
turelojya.context: Real client project at Owius, a bespoke development consultancy in Barcelona (owius.com). In a compact full-stack team I owned the platform end-to-end: MVC architecture on Node.js, React storefront, WordPress migration, payment integrations and Docker deployment with MongoDB.
turelojya.overview: TuRelojYa (turelojya.com) is a B2C e-commerce specialized in luxury watches with managed inventory, automatic valuation from price history, Redsys gateway, email invoicing and a proprietary admin panel. It replaces a legacy WordPress site with a modern stack focused on performance, SEO and autonomous business operations.

highlights.title: Inventory & data migration
highlights.text: Exported and normalized the catalog from WordPress into MongoDB. Designed the backend with model–controller–routes pattern and secure APIs with CORS configured for the frontend.

highlights.title: Automated valuation engine
highlights.text: Processed 100,000+ price records to deliver reliable real-time valuations. Implemented daily scraping and optimized the service to avoid overloading server resources.

highlights.title: Frontend, performance & SEO
highlights.text: Responsive React UI with Lighthouse focus: optimized images, sitemaps, dynamic metadata and multilingual URLs (i18n) for correct Google indexing.

highlights.title: Payments, email & backoffice
highlights.text: Integrated Redsys for checkout, Brevo for transactional email and PDF invoices, and a custom admin panel so the client manages orders and users without technical support.

highlights.title: Deployment & security
highlights.text: Migrated the application between servers with Docker, set up production MongoDB and applied security best practices (password hashing, secrets management, Compass for ops).

turelojya.stackIntro: Full-stack chosen to scale catalog, payments and SEO.
turelojya.role: Junior full-stack developer (company internship): backend and frontend design and implementation, valuation algorithm and scraping pipeline, payment integration, technical SEO, backoffice, deployment and direct client communication to iterate on business requirements.
turelojya.caseStudyTitle: Case study: TuRelojYa — Luxury watch e-commerce
turelojya.caseStudyIntro: TuRelojYa is a production B2C e-commerce for premium watch trading, with automatic valuation engine, Redsys payment gateway and multilingual SEO — developed end-to-end during my internship at Owius.
turelojya.caseStudyProblem: The client operated with a slow legacy WordPress site, hard to maintain and without automatic valuation or modern checkout. They needed a scalable platform managing inventory, valuing watches with market data and operating autonomously.
turelojya.caseStudySolution: Migrated catalog to MongoDB with Node.js backend (MVC), responsive React storefront and valuation engine over 100,000+ price records with optimized daily scraping. Integrated Redsys payments, Brevo transactional emails, custom admin panel and technical SEO with sitemaps, dynamic metadata and i18n.
turelojya.caseStudyResults: Platform deployed in production at turelojya.com with Docker, autonomous business operation and optimized Lighthouse scores. Real full-stack e-commerce experience at a Barcelona consultancy, demonstrable for teams seeking a productive profile from day one.

evenely.heroSummary: SaaS platform to design virtual escape rooms without code: visual slide editor, real-time collaboration, Free/Basic/Premium subscriptions with recurring Redsys billing and a separate play web with rankings and analytics — the capstone of my Owius internship.
evenely.context: Capstone of my internship at Owius (owius.com): the most demanding technical challenge of the period. I built from scratch a product that lets companies and trainers create gamified experiences without programming, with a Canva/PowerPoint-inspired visual editor and a multi-app architecture (portal, admin and play).
evenely.overview: Evenely (evenely.com) is a virtual escape room builder for corporate events, training, recruitment and internal comms. Clients design experiences by placing elements (text, images, video, buttons and quizzes) on slides; players join via a room code on a separate web that mirrors the design faithfully, with live rankings and participation data for organizers.

highlights.title: Visual slide editor
highlights.text: Users add pages and place elements freely. Each component can trigger actions: jump to another screen, open modals or validate answers in real time.

highlights.title: Live collaboration
highlights.text: Multiple editors can work on the same escape room simultaneously. I managed shared state to avoid conflicts between users and keep the canvas consistent.

highlights.title: Responsive from the canvas
highlights.text: The output must look good on mobile, tablet and desktop even when designed by non-technical users dragging elements freely — one of the trickiest product challenges.

highlights.title: Subscriptions & recurring Redsys
highlights.text: Free, Basic and Premium plans with automatic recurring charges via card tokenization in Redsys, plus security hardening for communications and token storage.

highlights.title: Editor portal vs play app
highlights.text: Separated the builder (portal.evenely.com) from the play web (app.evenely.com): creators edit while participants only enter a code. Includes live rankings and progress analytics.

evenely.stackIntro: Multi-app architecture with shared backend, focused on real time, payments and visual fidelity between editor and play.

stack.name: React
stack.role: Client portal, admin panel & play app

stack.name: TypeScript
stack.role: Typing across frontend and shared services

evenely.role: Junior full-stack developer (company internship): visual editor design, interactivity logic, collaborative editing, Redsys subscription integration, portal/play separation, rankings and analytics, and payment-flow security hardening.

react-course.heroSummary: Interactive React course for beginners: 30 levels guiding you to build a real portfolio, with local auto-checks and cloud progress.
react-course.context: I wanted a practical, step-by-step way to learn React with immediate feedback — not passive tutorials or copy-paste without understanding. I designed a gamified path inside this portfolio: each level explains concepts (JSX, components, props, state, hooks, routing…) while you build your site with Vite and Tailwind.
react-course.overview: Download a template, run npm run dev and npm run check. Playwright validates each step in your code; you advance one at a time. The course web shows React concepts, clear instructions and a level map. Complete all 30 for a diploma with your name.

highlights.title: 30 progressive levels
highlights.text: From Hello World to Vercel deploy: JSX, components, props, useState, useEffect, React Router, Context, Vitest tests and GitHub Actions CI.

highlights.title: Automatic checking
highlights.text: Playwright verifies your code locally. One step = one advance; no batch completion even if code already satisfies later steps.

highlights.title: Concepts explained
highlights.text: Each step includes a React concept explanation before the practical action — no need to hunt for hints.

highlights.title: Real portfolio outcome
highlights.text: End result is a deployable portfolio with navbar, projects, contact, theme toggle and admin panel.

react-course.role: Pedagogical design, 30 levels of content, verification engine, level map UI and portfolio integration.

ego.heroSummary: Electric mobility app (UPC): stations, range-aware routes, gamification and full backend with Stripe — published APK and deployable API.
ego.context: E-Go is the React Native course project at Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (2025). I turned course requirements into an ambitious product: not just a map app, but a platform with its own API, real charging data (ICAEN), range logic, subscriptions and an admin panel — plus docs, tests and cloud deployment.
ego.overview: EV and hybrid drivers find active stations, plan routes based on real range, save favorites and compete on leaderboards while charging. The Expo/React Native app consumes an Express + PostgreSQL API with Google OAuth, JWT, Stripe webhooks, geolocation and AWS jobs. Includes an Android APK and architecture ready to scale.

highlights.title: University project → real product
highlights.text: From coursework to downloadable app, documented API and CI-ready repo.

highlights.title: Maps & open data
highlights.text: Thousands of charging points (ICAEN), filters, favorites and clear mobile UI.

highlights.title: Routes & range (can-reach)
highlights.text: Reach calculation by vehicle and battery to recommend charging stops on real trips.

highlights.title: Gamification & retention
highlights.text: Rankings, live points and rewards (skins, ad-free premium) for engagement.

highlights.title: Backend & payments
highlights.text: Express, PostgreSQL, Stripe + webhooks, Lambda, S3, Docker, Jest/Supertest and GitHub Actions.

highlights.title: Expo mobile app
highlights.text: React Native, Expo Router, TypeScript, Google OAuth and APK on GitHub Releases.

ego.stackIntro: Mobile-first stack with custom backend and cloud deployment.
ego.role: Mobile UX/UI design, full-stack architecture, frontend and backend implementation, payments and maps integration, tests, documentation and UPC delivery.

portfolio.heroSummary: Live portfolio in production: own visual identity, i18n, adaptive performance and real embedded products (Study Agents, E-Go, games).
portfolio.context: I needed more than a static CV — a hub that shows product sense, code and visual craft. Built as a full Next.js app, not a template, linking academic, personal and professional work.
portfolio.overview: Multi-language site (es/en/it) with a cinematic home, skills, experience, education and filterable projects. Live demos, narrative detail pages and device-aware optimizations (WebGL only when it makes sense).

highlights.title: Design system
highlights.text: Teal palette, League Spartan, glass/cyan buttons and shared CSS across main routes.

highlights.title: Full i18n
highlights.text: next-intl, locale routes and consistent StaggeredMenu branding.

highlights.title: Adaptive performance
highlights.text: Hyperspeed/Orb/WebGL toned down on mobile and reduced-motion.

highlights.title: Embedded products
highlights.text: Study Agents in-repo; links to E-Go, games and external demos from one hub.

highlights.title: Project storytelling
highlights.text: Grid listing, area filters and recruiter-oriented detail pages.

portfolio.stackIntro: Built for SSR, SEO and continuous deployment.
portfolio.role: Concept, UX/UI, frontend development, project integration, i18n, performance and repository maintenance.

shooter.heroSummary: Unreal multiplayer shooter (Salmon Infinite): custom characters, networking and pro 3D pipeline — part 2 of my online games line.
shooter.context: After Salmon (part 1), I raised the bar: better visuals, custom animation and stable online sessions. A personal project showing growth in Unreal, 3D art and Game Jolt release.
shooter.overview: Third-person shooter with PBR materials, rigged characters and online matches. Full Blender → Substance → Mixamo pipeline, Blueprints/C++ gameplay and a public build anyone can try.

highlights.title: Online multiplayer
highlights.text: Networked sessions, player sync and a playable match loop.

highlights.title: Art & materials
highlights.text: Modeling, Substance Painter textures and realistic Unreal look.

highlights.title: Character animation
highlights.text: Mixamo rigs adapted to gameplay.

highlights.title: Release
highlights.text: Game Jolt build linked from the portfolio.

shooter.stackIntro: Unreal engine plus classic indie 3D art pipeline.
shooter.role: Creative direction, gameplay programming, 3D art, online integration and release.

tire-inflator.heroSummary: Tire inflation simulator in Proteus with C firmware — UPC embedded systems project.
tire-inflator.context: UPC embedded systems course: model a real control system in simulation before hardware. Documented requirements, schematic and inflater behavior.
tire-inflator.overview: Proteus circuit simulating sensors, actuators and safe inflation logic. C firmware with clear states (inflate, stop, alerts) and edge-case validation in simulation.

highlights.title: Schematic & simulation
highlights.text: Full electrical design and Proteus testing.

highlights.title: C firmware
highlights.text: Pressure control, timers and inflation safety.

highlights.title: Documentation
highlights.text: Technical report aligned with course criteria.

tire-inflator.role: System design, firmware implementation and simulation validation for university delivery.

fiblab.heroSummary: FIB Lab: 2D Unity game from the essential Unity course (UPC) — published on itch.io.
fiblab.context: First formal Unity course at FIB: demonstrate 2D physics, UI, scene management and a complete game loop on a tight deadline.
fiblab.overview: 2D platformer with enemies, scoring and menus. Custom Photoshop assets, organized scenes and a Web/PC build for evaluators and friends.

highlights.title: 2D gameplay
highlights.text: Full mechanics, collisions and level progression.

highlights.title: Custom art
highlights.text: Sprites and UI in Photoshop.

highlights.title: itch.io release
highlights.text: Playable online build linked from the portfolio.

fiblab.role: Full game development for course delivery: design, code, art and release.

circusvr.heroSummary: Circus VR: circus experience in virtual reality — Game Jam BCN 2024 (48h).
circusvr.context: Multidisciplinary team, one weekend, circus theme. My role: Unreal VR logic, interactions and fast Blender assets to keep jam pace.
circusvr.overview: Playable VR prototype with acrobatics, audience and circus setting. Focus on presence, visual/haptic feedback and a vertical slice demoable at jam end.

highlights.title: Unreal VR
highlights.text: Interactions, locomotion and circus scenario.

highlights.title: Fast 3D assets
highlights.text: Blender models optimized for VR.

highlights.title: Jam delivery
highlights.text: Build presented at Game Jam BCN with process notes.

circusvr.role: VR programming, supporting 3D art and technical coordination during the jam.

vr-experience.heroSummary: Research project (4th ESO): first VR experience on the metaverse with Unreal and MetaHumans.
vr-experience.context: High-school research: explain the metaverse through an immersive demo, not only a written report. My first serious contact with Unreal and VR.
vr-experience.overview: Explorable virtual world with MetaHuman characters, careful lighting and a guided tour for oral presentation. Combined written research with a playable Game Jolt build.

highlights.title: VR world
highlights.text: Navigable scene with spatial storytelling.

highlights.title: MetaHumans
highlights.text: Believable characters integrated in Unreal.

drovo.heroSummary: DROVO: fun Unreal multiplayer prototype to play with friends — fast iteration and online release.
drovo.context: I wanted a light social game, not AAA scope: build in weeks, test with friends and publish. DROVO experiments with game design and basic networking.
drovo.overview: Online prototype with cartoon characters, compact map and short sessions. Blender → Mixamo → Unreal pipeline prioritizing fun over hyper-realistic graphics.

highlights.title: Multiplayer
highlights.text: Online sessions with friends.

highlights.title: Visual style
highlights.text: Colorful, readable look in Blender.

highlights.title: Game Jolt
highlights.text: Public build to download and play.

drovo.role: Ideation, prototype development, 3D art and release.

salmon1.heroSummary: Salmon Infinite (part 1): my first multiplayer game — where it all started (2020).
salmon1.context: First serious programming and games project: learn Unreal, C++, modeling and publish something playable online. Part 2 (shooter) shows evolution years later.
salmon1.overview: Experimental multiplayer with simple characters, maps and basic Unreal networking. Full pipeline: model, texture, implement logic and upload to Game Jolt.

highlights.text: Unreal + C++ from scratch as a teenager.

3dprinting.heroSummary: Large-scale 3D printing: CAD, slicer and functional parts — personal maker project.
3dprinting.context: Beyond software: real parts via CAD, Cura prep and FDM validation.
3dprinting.overview: Large-format pieces with tuned orientation, supports, infill and post-processing. FreeCAD for parametrics, Blender for remeshing when needed, Cura for reliable profiles.

highlights.text: Adapted parts with real measurements in FreeCAD.

highlights.title: Advanced slicing
highlights.text: UltiMaker Cura profiles for large parts.

highlights.title: Physical finishing
highlights.text: Sanding, assembly and tolerance checks.

experience.title: EXPERIENCE
experience.tagline: My professional path in software development and technology.
experience.seoDescription: Pau Pedrejon's professional experience: company full-stack work, UPC projects and real shipped products. Built for recruiters and engineering teams.

owius.title: Software development internship
owius.summary:
Full-stack web development since November: data-heavy products, e-commerce with payments, admin tooling, WordPress, and Evenely, a SaaS to build virtual escape rooms.

Typical stack: React, React Native, Node, Redsys, cron jobs, SEO, i18n, and performance tuning.

workBlocks.title: E-commerce + operations console
points: Joined a large ongoing project; now live in production.
Daily automated scraping (cron) on a 100k+ row dataset.
Grouping, filtering, and dropping rows based on business rules.
Checkout with Redsys plus bank transfer invoicing (PDF).
CRM-style backoffice for catalog, orders, and internal ops.

workBlocks.title: Corporate site
points: Short engagement (~3 days): fixes, tweaks, and small features.

workBlocks.title: Virtual escape room
points: Moved from hard-coded content to an editable flow—no redeploy for copy changes.
Screens, question banks, and quizzes managed from the backoffice.

workBlocks.title: WordPress
points: Responsive layout on an existing theme.
Updates to a custom PHP widget.

workBlocks.title: Evenely — escape room builder
points: Greenfield SaaS: slide-style editor with interactivity (pages, buttons, quizzes, image and video).
Live collaboration, monthly subscription portal (recurring Redsys & tokenization) and security hardening.
Separate play web: room code, live ranking and analytics for organizers.

experience.yearsFootnote: * Internship in progress; end date may align with academic or contract terms.

contact.seoDescription: Contact Pau Pedrejon for job opportunities, freelance work or software and AI collaborations.

faq.question: Are you available for new opportunities?
faq.answer: Yes, I'm open to job opportunities, internships, freelance work and collaborations in software development and AI, in Barcelona or remote.

faq.question: What is your main tech stack?
faq.answer: React, Next.js, TypeScript on the frontend; Node.js and Python on the backend; experience with AI/LLMs (LangChain, RAG, agents), MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Docker and production deployment.

faq.question: Where are you located?
faq.answer: I live in Castelldefels (Barcelona, Spain). I can work on-site in the Barcelona metropolitan area or remotely.

faq.question: What kind of projects interest you?
faq.answer: Full-stack projects with real impact: SaaS, e-commerce, AI platforms, mobile apps and developer productivity tools.

faq.question: Can I see your CV?
faq.answer: Yes, you can download my CV from the portfolio homepage. You can also contact me directly via email or LinkedIn.

consent.title: Cookies & analytics
consent.description: We use first-party analytics to measure visits. They are only enabled if you accept the

privacy.title: Privacy & cookies
privacy.tagline: How personal data is handled on this website.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body: Pau Pedrejon (paupedrejon@gmail.com) is the data controller for personal data collected through this portfolio.

data.title: Data we collect
data.body:
If you accept analytics cookies, we may record: pages visited, language, referrer, anonymous session ID (UUID in your browser), event type (e.g. CV download or project view), and basic technical visit data.

I do not intentionally collect name, email, or IP in first-party analytics, except what may indirectly arise from network traffic.

purpose.title: Purpose and legal basis
purpose.body:
The purpose is to obtain usage statistics to improve the portfolio. The legal basis is your consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a) and ePrivacy rules).

If you reject, analytics is not enabled and the session identifier is not stored for that purpose.

cookies.title: Cookies and local storage
cookies.body:
We use localStorage to remember your consent choice and, if you accept, an anonymous session ID (portfolio_sid).

Vercel Analytics may also be enabled if you accept; it uses cookies or similar technologies under its own policy.

Strictly necessary cookies for Study Agents sign-in (if you use it) are separate and managed within that service.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body: Analytics events are kept for up to 24 months, unless a shorter erasure is requested or required by law.

recipients.title: Processors and transfers
recipients.body:
Data may be stored in Supabase (database) and, if you accept analytics, processed by Vercel Analytics. These providers act as data processors.

International transfers may occur depending on their infrastructure; appropriate safeguards (e.g. standard contractual clauses) apply where relevant.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body:
You may withdraw consent at any time by clearing this site's data in your browser.

You also have rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction, and portability, and may lodge a complaint with your supervisory authority.
